expectations.text: Evidence the agent read the actual current SKILL.md (references current wording correctly, does not hallucinate)
expectations.evidence: Quotes the current description verbatim in a code block ("做 skill（Claude Code 技能）相关的工作时进入——从零写一个新 skill...") and correctly cites §0/§1/§3/§4 structure matching the actual file.

expectations.text: No files were modified — only a proposal was produced
expectations.evidence: Process-notes states "本任务要求不改文件，只出 proposal" and only proposal.md + process-notes.md were written to the outputs directory.

expectations.text: Acknowledges that skill changes do not take effect in the current session (content is loaded once per session)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states "当前会话不生效。SKILL.md 一次会话只加载一次...要下次会话才会用新的" under the caveat section.

expectations.text: Proposed description is actually shorter than the original
expectations.evidence: Original 228 chars → proposed ~130 chars, explicitly noted as "比原来短约 43%".

expectations.text: Proposed description retains the key trigger categories: creating a new skill, optimizing an existing skill, self-optimization
expectations.evidence: New text includes "新建、调优、把反复流程沉淀成 skill、修触发不对的 description，或者读自己的文件优化 skill-master" covering all three categories.

expectations.text: Explicitly enumerates what was retained and what was dropped, with reasoning
expectations.evidence: Contains dedicated sections "保留（每个都是不可妥协的触发场景）" and "丢弃（冗余或低价值）" with per-item reasoning.

expectations.text: Treats this as a proposal-first operation (per §4 self-optimization guidance), not a bolt-the-barn-door-shut edit
expectations.evidence: Explicitly references §4.2 step 4 "提出方案先讲、别直接改" and ends with "需要用户确认的点" asking for user confirmation before action.
